Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 37 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Show HN: Claude skill that evaluates B2B vendors by talking to their AI agents 33 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 57 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Personal Encyclopedias 678 upvotes, 136 comments
  5. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 50 upvotes, 6 comments
  6. Building a Blog with Elixir and Phoenix 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Obsolete Sounds 161 upvotes, 33 comments
  8. Interoperability Can Save the Open Web (2023) 138 upvotes, 38 comments
  9. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 789 upvotes, 273 comments
  10. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 220 upvotes, 69 comments
  11. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 62 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 19 upvotes, 3 comments
  13. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  14. Doom entirely from DNS records 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 392 upvotes, 186 comments
  16. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 361 upvotes, 170 comments
  17. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 91 upvotes, 36 comments
  18. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 107 upvotes, 50 comments
  19. Niche Museums 85 upvotes, 39 comments
  20. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 206 upvotes, 110 comments
  21. End of "Chat Control": EU parliament stops mass surveillance 344 upvotes, 198 comments
  22. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 18 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. Swift 6.3 266 upvotes, 160 comments
  24. ARC-AGI-3 477 upvotes, 306 comments
  25. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  27. AI users whose lives were wrecked by delusion 115 upvotes, 103 comments
  28. Optimizing a lock-free ring buffer 56 upvotes, 53 comments
  29. French e, è, é, ê, ë – what's the difference? 83 upvotes, 88 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
